on this feedback there are mutiple issues that are need to be fixed can u fix thoses

1	# Delivery Acceptance & Project Architecture Audit (Static-Only)
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-12
4	Repository: repo
5	Audit mode: Static-only (no runtime execution)
6	
7	## 1. Verdict
8	- Overall conclusion: Partial Pass
9	
10	## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
11	- What was reviewed:
12	  - Documentation, manifests, env/config, Docker artifacts, route registration, core modules, migrations/seeds, scripts, and tests.
13	  - Primary evidence sources include README.md:5, package.json:13, src/bootstrap/register-routes.js:30, db/migrations/20260410_004_p2_university_versioned_entities.js:50, tests/integration/rankings.pipeline.spec.js:148.
14	- What was not reviewed:
15	  - Runtime behavior under real load, live container orchestration behavior, external environment/network behavior.
16	- What was intentionally not executed:
17	  - Project startup, Docker, tests, migrations, scripts.
18	- Claims requiring manual verification:
19	  - p95 latency and throughput target compliance (README.md:128, docs/observability.md:65, docs/observability.md:70).
20	  - Session rotation behavior under high-concurrency lock contention (src/modules/auth/session.service.js:68, src/modules/auth/session.service.js:73).
21	  - Retention purge script execution correctness in PostgreSQL (scripts/purge-expired-data.js:38, scripts/purge-expired-data.js:43).
22	
23	## 3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
24	- Prompt core goal mapped:
25	  - University versioned master data, review assignment/scoring/aggregation, search, and personalization are all present and wired under /v1 route groups (src/bootstrap/register-routes.js:35, src/bootstrap/register-routes.js:37, src/bootstrap/register-routes.js:39, src/bootstrap/register-routes.js:40).
26	- Major constraints mapped:
27	  - One-active-version DB enforcement exists (db/migrations/20260410_004_p2_university_versioned_entities.js:50).
28	  - COI checks include institution window and prior-cycle logic (src/modules/reviews/assignments/coi.service.js:30, src/modules/reviews/assignments/coi.service.js:51).
29	  - Scoring bounds and step are validated (src/modules/reviews/scoring/route.js:14).
30	  - Ranking tie-breakers are deterministic (src/modules/rankings/aggregation.service.js:171, src/modules/rankings/aggregation.service.js:172, src/modules/rankings/aggregation.service.js:173).
31	  - Search synonym expansion and highlighting implemented (src/modules/search/search.service.js:29, src/modules/search/search.service.js:102).
32	- Significant requirement-fit gaps found:
33	  - Idempotency not persisted for successful 204/no-body writes.
34	  - Audit trail is not complete across all create/update write paths.
35	  - Personalization similarity implementation is narrow relative to prompt.
36	
37	## 4. Section-by-section Review
38	
39	### 4.1 Hard Gates
40	
41	#### 4.1.1 Documentation and static verifiability
42	- Conclusion: Pass
43	- Rationale: Startup/config/test instructions and entrypoint wiring are statically traceable.
44	- Evidence: README.md:5, README.md:19, README.md:91, package.json:13, package.json:21, .env.example:12, .env.example:35, src/bootstrap/register-routes.js:30.
45	- Manual verification note: Runtime correctness remains out of scope.
46	
47	#### 4.1.2 Material deviation from Prompt
48	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
49	- Rationale: Architecture and modules align with prompt scope, but several explicit requirement semantics are only partially met (idempotency breadth, full audit trail breadth, personalization similarity depth).
50	- Evidence: src/common/idempotency/idempotency.middleware.js:72, README.md:133, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:161, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:251.
51	
52	### 4.2 Delivery Completeness
53	
54	#### 4.2.1 Coverage of explicit core requirements
55	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
56	- Rationale: Most core functional areas exist and are implemented; key non-functional/semantic requirements have material gaps.
57	- Evidence: src/modules/university-data/_versioning/versioned-repository.factory.js:166, src/modules/reviews/assignments/coi.service.js:68, src/modules/reviews/attachments/attachment.service.js:63, src/modules/rankings/aggregation.service.js:87, src/modules/search/search.service.js:29.
58	
59	#### 4.2.2 End-to-end deliverable completeness (0→1)
60	- Conclusion: Pass
61	- Rationale: Repo structure, migrations, seeds, scripts, and tests represent a real service delivery rather than a code fragment.
62	- Evidence: README.md:31, db/migrations/20260410_000_p0_baseline_extensions.js:1, db/seeds/00_roles_permissions.js:1, src/server.js:1, tests/integration/scoring.submit.spec.js:1.
63	
64	### 4.3 Engineering and Architecture Quality
65	
66	#### 4.3.1 Structure and decomposition quality
67	- Conclusion: Pass
68	- Rationale: Modules are separated by capability and route wiring is explicit.
69	- Evidence: src/bootstrap/register-routes.js:32, src/bootstrap/register-routes.js:35, src/bootstrap/register-routes.js:37, src/bootstrap/register-routes.js:39.
70	
71	#### 4.3.2 Maintainability/extensibility
72	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
73	- Rationale: Core structure is maintainable, but inconsistent API patch shapes and missing param validation in several routes increase maintenance and defect risk.
74	- Evidence: src/modules/search/route.js:94, src/modules/search/route.js:98, src/modules/search/saved-queries.service.js:36, src/modules/university-data/_versioning/versioned.route.factory.js:7, src/modules/university-data/_versioning/versioned.route.factory.js:58, src/modules/university-data/_versioning/versioned.route.factory.js:117.
75	
76	### 4.4 Engineering Details and Professionalism
77	
78	#### 4.4.1 Error handling/logging/validation/API design
79	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
80	- Rationale: Good error envelope and logging baseline exists, but material issues remain in idempotency breadth, audit breadth, and some validation consistency.
81	- Evidence: src/common/errors/error-handler.middleware.js:17, src/common/logging/logger.js:15, src/common/logging/logger.js:17, src/common/idempotency/idempotency.middleware.js:72, src/modules/search/route.js:94.
82	
83	#### 4.4.2 Product-like service quality
84	- Conclusion: Pass
85	- Rationale: Service is organized as a production-style backend with DB migrations, RBAC, observability, and modular business domains.
86	- Evidence: README.md:3, README.md:78, docs/architecture.md:1, src/app.js:24.
87	
88	### 4.5 Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit
89	
90	#### 4.5.1 Business-goal and constraint fit
91	- Conclusion: Partial Pass
92	- Rationale: Core admissions workflow and supporting modules are implemented; specific prompt semantics are partially fulfilled in personalization similarity and full-write idempotency/audit breadth.
93	- Evidence: src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:161, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:252, src/common/idempotency/idempotency.middleware.js:72, README.md:133.
94	
95	### 4.6 Aesthetics (frontend-only)
96	
97	#### 4.6.1 Visual/interaction quality
98	- Conclusion: Not Applicable
99	- Rationale: Delivery is backend-only.
100	- Evidence: README.md:3.
101	
102	## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)
103	
104	### High
105	
106	1) Severity: High
107	- Title: Idempotency protection is bypassed for successful write responses without body (e.g., 204)
108	- Conclusion: Fail
109	- Evidence: src/common/idempotency/idempotency.middleware.js:72, src/modules/personalization/route.js:31, src/modules/personalization/route.js:39, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:11
110	- Impact: Duplicate submissions can still create repeated side effects for authenticated write endpoints returning 204 (notably view history), violating prompt idempotency intent.
111	- Minimum actionable fix: Persist idempotency records for all successful authenticated writes (2xx), including 204 with a canonical empty response payload.
112	
113	2) Severity: High
114	- Title: Full audit trail requirement is not met across all create/update write paths
115	- Conclusion: Fail
116	- Evidence: README.md:133, src/modules/accounts/account.service.js:16, src/modules/accounts/account.service.js:37, src/modules/rbac/rbac.service.js:54, src/modules/rbac/rbac.service.js:61, src/modules/rbac/rbac.service.js:69, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:37, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:74, src/modules/search/saved-queries.service.js:19
117	- Impact: Important state changes occur without who/when/what audit entries, reducing compliance and forensic traceability.
118	- Minimum actionable fix: Introduce a mandatory audit decorator/pattern for every mutating service method and enforce via tests/lint rule.
119	
120	3) Severity: High
121	- Title: Personalization implementation does not satisfy prompt-level similarity depth
122	- Conclusion: Partial Fail
123	- Evidence: src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:161, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:162, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:209, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:251, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:253
124	- Impact: Recommendations are primarily heuristic boosts (views/bookmarks/entity_type tag) without clear tag/attribute similarity across broader entities, weakening requirement fit.
125	- Minimum actionable fix: Add explicit similarity scoring (for example tag overlap/Jaccard on entity attributes) and expand candidate entities beyond only universities/majors in cold-start.
126	
127	4) Severity: High
128	- Title: Suspected auth reliability risk under concurrency from FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
129	- Conclusion: Suspected Risk / Cannot Confirm Statistically
130	- Evidence: src/modules/auth/session.service.js:68, src/modules/auth/session.service.js:73, src/modules/auth/session.service.js:75
131	- Impact: Concurrent requests can transiently fail with 401 if token row is lock-skipped.
132	- Minimum actionable fix: Replace SKIP LOCKED with lock timeout + retry strategy, or retry once before returning AuthenticationError.
133	
134	### Medium
135	
136	5) Severity: Medium
137	- Title: Purge script uses likely-invalid PostgreSQL interval placeholder syntax
138	- Conclusion: Suspected Risk
139	- Evidence: scripts/purge-expired-data.js:38, scripts/purge-expired-data.js:43
140	- Impact: Retention cleanup may fail at runtime, causing data growth and retention-policy drift.
141	- Minimum actionable fix: Use parameter-safe interval arithmetic such as NOW() - (? * INTERVAL '1 day').
142	
143	6) Severity: Medium
144	- Title: Saved-query PATCH API shape mismatch and missing request validation
145	- Conclusion: Fail
146	- Evidence: src/modules/search/route.js:19, src/modules/search/route.js:82, src/modules/search/route.js:94, src/modules/search/route.js:98, src/modules/search/saved-queries.service.js:36
147	- Impact: Client PATCH payloads using queryText can be silently ignored; malformed patches are not schema-validated.
148	- Minimum actionable fix: Add PATCH schema with explicit allowed fields and map camelCase API contract fields to DB columns consistently.
149	
150	7) Severity: Medium
151	- Title: UUID path parameter validation is inconsistently applied
152	- Conclusion: Partial Fail
153	- Evidence: src/modules/university-data/_versioning/versioned.route.factory.js:7, src/modules/university-data/_versioning/versioned.route.factory.js:58, src/modules/university-data/_versioning/versioned.route.factory.js:117, src/modules/accounts/route.js:24, src/modules/accounts/account.service.js:10
154	- Impact: Malformed UUID path params may bubble into DB-layer errors (500) rather than clean 400 validation responses.
155	- Minimum actionable fix: Apply validate({ params: ... }) schemas uniformly on all UUID path-param routes.
156	
157	8) Severity: Medium
158	- Title: Audit view masking semantics do not match masked-view requirement
159	- Conclusion: Partial Fail
160	- Evidence: src/common/crypto/field-encryption.js:50, src/modules/admin/audit/audit.service.js:57
161	- Impact: Non-admin viewers receive null summaries instead of masked summaries, reducing audit usability and diverging from prompt wording.
162	- Minimum actionable fix: Use maskField-based selective masking for allowed viewers instead of nulling all summaries.
163	
164	9) Severity: Medium
165	- Title: Documentation inconsistency on audit-events access scope
166	- Conclusion: Fail (Documentation consistency)
167	- Evidence: docs/security-model.md:98, docs/permission-matrix.md:24, db/seeds/00_roles_permissions.js:85, db/seeds/00_roles_permissions.js:92, src/modules/admin/route.js:31
168	- Impact: Static verifiability is weakened; auditors cannot infer intended authorization policy reliably from docs.
169	- Minimum actionable fix: Align security-model doc, permission matrix, and seed policy to one intended access model.
170	
171	10) Severity: Medium
172	- Title: Search fielded filtering is narrower than prompt expectation
173	- Conclusion: Partial Fail
174	- Evidence: src/modules/search/route.js:12, src/modules/search/search.service.js:59, src/modules/search/search.service.js:74
175	- Impact: Filtering is effectively limited to entity type; richer fielded filtering capabilities are not evident.
176	- Minimum actionable fix: Extend query schema and SQL builder with additional fielded filters (for example lifecycle status, effective dates, and key payload fields).
177	
178	## 6. Security Review Summary
179	
180	- Authentication entry points
181	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
182	  - Evidence: src/modules/auth/route.js:11, src/modules/auth/auth.middleware.js:33, src/modules/auth/session.service.js:68
183	  - Reasoning: Entry points and bearer/session checks are explicit, but concurrency lock behavior introduces suspected reliability risk.
184	
185	- Route-level authorization
186	  - Conclusion: Pass
187	  - Evidence: src/modules/rbac/rbac.middleware.js:20, src/modules/rbac/rbac.middleware.js:29, src/modules/admin/route.js:12, src/modules/admin/route.js:31
188	  - Reasoning: Protected routes consistently apply capability checks.
189	
190	- Object-level authorization
191	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
192	  - Evidence: src/modules/applications/application.service.js:58, src/modules/reviews/assignments/assignment.service.js:251, src/modules/reviews/workbench/workbench.service.js:37, src/modules/reviews/attachments/attachment.service.js:52
193	  - Reasoning: Strong checks exist in critical reviewer/applicant flows; still requires broader route-by-route verification for total certainty.
194	
195	- Function-level authorization
196	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
197	  - Evidence: src/modules/reviews/scoring/scoring.service.js:56, src/modules/reviews/scoring/scoring.service.js:122, src/modules/reviews/assignments/assignment.service.js:38
198	  - Reasoning: Service-level guards are present for key review flows, but not universal across all mutating domains.
199	
200	- Tenant/user data isolation
201	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
202	  - Evidence: src/modules/applications/application.service.js:69, src/modules/personalization/personalization.service.js:26, src/modules/search/saved-queries.service.js:34
203	  - Reasoning: Isolation patterns are present for major per-user resources.
204	
205	- Admin/internal/debug endpoint protection
206	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
207	  - Evidence: src/modules/admin/route.js:12, src/modules/admin/route.js:31, docs/security-model.md:98, docs/permission-matrix.md:24
208	  - Reasoning: Admin endpoints are permission-gated, but policy/document mismatch remains on audit visibility scope.
209	
210	## 7. Tests and Logging Review
211	
212	- Unit tests
213	  - Conclusion: Pass
214	  - Evidence: tests/unit/idempotency.dedup.spec.js:92, tests/unit/auth.password-rules.spec.js:19, tests/unit/blind-modes.projection.spec.js:9, tests/unit/aggregation.trimmed-mean.spec.js:50
215	
216	- API/integration tests
217	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
218	  - Evidence: tests/api/auth.login.spec.js:11, tests/api/assignments.spec.js:41, tests/integration/scoring.submit.spec.js:187, tests/integration/rankings.pipeline.spec.js:148
219	  - Reasoning: Integration suites cover many core data paths; many API suites are service-mocked and do not validate full runtime stacks.
220	
221	- Logging categories / observability
222	  - Conclusion: Pass
223	  - Evidence: src/common/logging/logger.js:11, src/common/metrics/metrics.js:15, src/common/metrics/metrics.js:38, src/modules/admin/route.js:12
224	
225	- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs/responses
226	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
227	  - Evidence: src/common/logging/logger.js:15, src/common/logging/logger.js:17, src/app.js:47, docs/requirement-traceability.md:58
228	  - Reasoning: Redaction exists, but no dedicated automated redaction tests are evident.
229	
230	## 8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)
231	
232	### 8.1 Test Overview
233	- Unit tests exist: yes.
234	- API tests exist: yes.
235	- Integration tests exist: yes.
236	- Frameworks: Vitest + Supertest.
237	- Test entry points: package.json scripts and vitest config.
238	- Documentation for test commands: present.
239	- Evidence: package.json:18, package.json:19, package.json:20, package.json:21, vitest.config.js:6, README.md:88, README.md:91.
240	
241	### 8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
242	
243	| Requirement / Risk Point | Mapped Test Case(s) | Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock | Coverage Assessment | Gap | Minimum Test Addition |
244	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
245	| Auth login + basic auth API responses | tests/api/auth.login.spec.js:78, tests/api/auth.login.spec.js:122 | Service/session are mocked (tests/api/auth.login.spec.js:11, tests/api/auth.login.spec.js:21) | Basically covered | No DB-backed session lifecycle or rotation coverage | Add integration tests for login/logout/session rotation and token grace-window behavior |
246	| Route-level 401/403 guard behavior | tests/api/rbac.route-guards.spec.js:63, tests/api/rbac.route-guards.spec.js:80 | Explicit 401/403 scenarios | Sufficient (route layer) | Does not prove deep service authorization correctness | Add end-to-end route+DB authorization tests for critical admin routes |
247	| Application object-level authorization | tests/integration/applications.submission.spec.js:206 | 403 for non-owner path in real DB-backed service | Sufficient | None material observed | Keep regression tests for role edge cases |
248	| Assignment object-level authorization | tests/integration/assignment-object-auth.spec.js:132 | 404 info-safe denial for non-owner reviewer | Sufficient | None material observed | Keep and extend to list endpoint edge cases |
249	| COI institution-window enforcement | tests/integration/coi.institution-window.spec.js:78 | Real SQL join + date-window behavior | Basically covered | Prior-cycle COI path lacks DB-backed integration evidence | Add integration test for prior-cycle reviewer block |
250	| Score step/range validation (0.5, 0–10) | tests/api/scores.spec.js:98 | 400 on invalid half-step values | Sufficient (route validation) | No HTTP+DB integration for malformed payload persistence safety | Add integration test that malformed payload never reaches DB writes |
251	| Submit scoring transitions | tests/integration/scoring.submit.spec.js:187 | Verifies score persistence, assignment state transition, reviewer load decrement | Sufficient | None material observed | Add concurrency submission conflict case |
252	| Aggregation escalation threshold | tests/integration/aggregation.escalation.spec.js:80 | Escalation event created when variance exceeds threshold | Sufficient | None material observed | Add boundary test at exactly threshold |
253	| Ranking deterministic tie-breakers | tests/integration/rankings.pipeline.spec.js:148 | Asserts order by mean then research fit | Sufficient | None material observed | Add explicit tie on submitted_at to validate tertiary tie handling |
254	| Search synonyms and ranking | tests/integration/search.fts.spec.js:133 | Synonym expansion path validated in DB-backed tests | Basically covered | Fielded filtering breadth not covered | Add integration tests for additional field filters |
255	| Version one-active enforcement | tests/integration/versioning.one-active-enforcement.spec.js:26 | Partial unique index behavior validated | Sufficient | No full publish workflow integration for all 8 entities | Add matrix tests for publish/activate/archive across all entity types |
256	| Idempotency dedup/conflict | tests/unit/idempotency.dedup.spec.js:92, tests/unit/idempotency.dedup.spec.js:117 | Unit-only middleware behavior | Insufficient | No integration coverage for 204/no-body write idempotency | Add API/integration tests for repeated key on /v1/personalization/views and DELETE endpoints |
257	| Logging redaction | docs/requirement-traceability.md:58 | No dedicated tests listed | Missing | Sensitive logging regressions may pass unnoticed | Add logger snapshot tests with password/token payloads |
258	| Personalization recommendations quality | tests/api/personalization.spec.js:82 | Service mocked in API tests | Insufficient | No DB-backed tests for similarity, declared-interest cold start, or popularity ordering | Add integration recommendation tests with seeded signals and expected ranking reasons |
259	
260	### 8.3 Security Coverage Audit
261	- Authentication
262	  - Conclusion: Insufficient
263	  - Evidence: tests/api/auth.login.spec.js:11, tests/api/auth.login.spec.js:21
264	  - Reason: Core auth/session behavior is heavily mocked in API tests; real rotation and timeout paths are not meaningfully covered.
265	
266	- Route authorization
267	  - Conclusion: Basically covered
268	  - Evidence: tests/api/rbac.route-guards.spec.js:63, tests/api/rbac.route-guards.spec.js:80
269	  - Reason: 401/403 route-guard behavior is tested.
270	
271	- Object-level authorization
272	  - Conclusion: Sufficient for key flows
273	  - Evidence: tests/integration/applications.submission.spec.js:206, tests/integration/assignment-object-auth.spec.js:132, tests/integration/attachments.service.spec.js:217
274	  - Reason: Real DB-backed checks exist for high-risk ownership boundaries.
275	
276	- Tenant/data isolation
277	  - Conclusion: Basically covered
278	  - Evidence: tests/integration/applications.submission.spec.js:235, tests/api/search.spec.js:177
279	  - Reason: Some per-user isolation paths are covered; broader cross-module isolation still not exhaustive.
280	
281	- Admin/internal protection
282	  - Conclusion: Basically covered
283	  - Evidence: tests/api/rbac.route-guards.spec.js:91, tests/api/rankings.spec.js:129
284	  - Reason: Admin route guard behavior is tested, but policy/document mismatch remains.
285	
286	### 8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
287	- Partial Pass
288	- Covered major risks:
289	  - Object-level authorization for key review/application paths.
290	  - Core scoring, aggregation, ranking, and version-constraint logic.
291	- Uncovered or weakly covered risks:
292	  - Real auth/session rotation concurrency behavior.
293	  - Idempotency behavior for 204/no-body writes.
294	  - Logging redaction regression detection.
295	  - Full personalization similarity/quality behavior under real DB data.
296	- Boundary statement:
297	  - Current tests could still pass while severe defects remain in auth/session concurrency, write-idempotency breadth, and logging-redaction assurance.
298	
299	## 9. Final Notes
300	- This report is static-only and evidence-bound; no runtime claims are asserted beyond static code/test artifacts.
301	- No code modifications were made.
302	- Highest-priority remediation should target idempotency breadth, complete audit coverage, and personalization requirement-fit gaps before acceptance.
Let me explore the repository structure to understand what exists before making fixes.

Explore the repository at C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t101\repo and read the following files in full. Report the complete content of each file (with line numbers). These are needed to fix issues identified in an audit:

1. src/common/idempotency/idempotency.middleware.js
2. src/modules/auth/session.service.js
3. scripts/purge-expired-data.js
4. src/modules/search/route.js
5. src/modules/search/saved-queries.service.js
6. src/modules/university-data/_versioning/versioned.route.factory.js
7. src/modules/accounts/route.js

1	import knex from '../../common/db/knex.js';
2	import { searchConfig } from '../../config/search.js';
3	import { searchQueriesTotal } from '../../common/metrics/metrics.js';
4	
5	const { tsConfig, headline, activeVersionBoost, defaultPageSize, maxPageSize } = searchConfig;
6	
7	/**
8	 * Full-text search across all versioned university-data entities.
9	 *
10	 * Uses PostgreSQL's ts_rank_cd (cover density ranking) to score results.
11	 * Only active versions are searched by default; archived/superseded are excluded.
12	 */
13	export const searchService = {
14	  /**
15	   * Search across all entity types.
16	   *
17	   * @param {string} queryText
18	   * @param {object} options
19	   * @param {string[]} [options.entityTypes]  Filter to specific entity types
20	   * @param {number}  [options.page]
21	   * @param {number}  [options.pageSize]
22	   * @param {string}  [options.accountId]     For logging
23	   * @param {string}  [options.requestId]
24	   */
25	  /**
26	   * Expand a query term by looking up its synonyms in the search_synonyms table.
27	   * Returns an array of all terms (original + synonyms, deduplicated).
28	   */
29	  async _expandSynonyms(queryText) {
30	    const terms = queryText
31	      .toLowerCase()
32	      .split(/\s+/)
33	      .map((t) => t.replace(/[^a-z0-9]/gi, '').trim())
34	      .filter(Boolean);
35	
36	    if (!terms.length) return [queryText];
37	
38	    const rows = await knex('search_synonyms').whereIn('term', terms).select('term', 'synonyms');
39	    const synonymMap = new Map(rows.map((r) => [r.term, r.synonyms]));
40	
41	    const expanded = new Set([queryText]);
42	    for (const term of terms) {
43	      const synonyms = synonymMap.get(term) || [];
44	      for (const s of synonyms) {
45	        expanded.add(s);
46	      }
47	    }
48	    return [...expanded];
49	  },
50	
51	  async search(queryText, options = {}) {
52	    if (!queryText?.trim()) return { rows: [], total: 0, queryText };
53	    searchQueriesTotal.inc();
54	
55	    const pageSize = Math.min(options.pageSize || defaultPageSize, maxPageSize);
56	    const page = options.page || 1;
57	    const offset = (page - 1) * pageSize;
58	
59	    const entityTypes = options.entityTypes?.length ? options.entityTypes : null;
60	
61	    // Expand query with synonyms for richer recall
62	    const expandedTerms = await this._expandSynonyms(queryText);
63	
64	    // We use a UNION ALL across entity version tables — each entity emits the same columns
65	    const entityConfigs = [
66	      { table: 'university_versions',            entityType: 'university',            idCol: 'university_id' },
67	      { table: 'school_versions',                entityType: 'school',                idCol: 'school_id' },
68	      { table: 'major_versions',                 entityType: 'major',                 idCol: 'major_id' },
69	      { table: 'research_track_versions',        entityType: 'research_track',        idCol: 'research_track_id' },
70	      { table: 'enrollment_plan_versions',       entityType: 'enrollment_plan',       idCol: 'enrollment_plan_id' },
71	      { table: 'transfer_quota_versions',        entityType: 'transfer_quota',        idCol: 'transfer_quota_id' },
72	      { table: 'application_requirement_versions', entityType: 'application_requirement', idCol: 'application_requirement_id' },
73	      { table: 'retest_rule_versions',           entityType: 'retest_rule',           idCol: 'retest_rule_id' },
74	    ].filter((e) => !entityTypes || entityTypes.includes(e.entityType));
75	
76	    if (!entityConfigs.length) return { rows: [], total: 0, queryText };
77	
78	    const start = Date.now();
79	
80	    // Build a combined tsquery from the original term and all synonym expansions.
81	    // Each term becomes a websearch_to_tsquery clause joined with ||.
82	    // websearch_to_tsquery supports quoted phrases and exclusions; it also uses
83	    // the grad_search config (unaccent + english_stem) for normalisation.
84	    const tsqueryExpr = expandedTerms
85	      .map(() => `websearch_to_tsquery(?, ?)`)
86	      .join(' || ');
87	    const tsqueryParams = expandedTerms.flatMap((t) => [tsConfig, t]);
88	
89	    // Build UNION ALL query
90	    const unionParts = entityConfigs.map(({ table, entityType, idCol }) =>
91	      knex.raw(
92	        `
93	        SELECT
94	          ? AS entity_type,
95	          ${idCol} AS stable_id,
96	          id AS version_id,
97	          lifecycle_status,
98	          payload_json->>'name' AS name,
99	          payload_json->>'description' AS description,
100	          ts_rank_cd(search_vector, (${tsqueryExpr}))
101	            * CASE WHEN lifecycle_status = 'active' THEN ${activeVersionBoost} ELSE 1.0 END AS rank,
102	          ts_headline(
103	            ?,
104	            payload_json->>'name' || ' ' || COALESCE(payload_json->>'description', ''),
105	            (${tsqueryExpr}),
106	            ?
107	          ) AS headline
108	        FROM ${table}
109	        WHERE search_vector @@ (${tsqueryExpr})
110	          AND lifecycle_status IN ('active', 'scheduled', 'superseded')
111	        `,
112	        [
113	          entityType,
114	          ...tsqueryParams,
115	          tsConfig,
116	          ...tsqueryParams,
117	          `StartSel=${headline.startSel},StopSel=${headline.stopSel},MaxWords=${headline.maxWords},MinWords=${headline.minWords}`,
118	          ...tsqueryParams,
119	        ],
120	      ).toString()
121	    );
122	
123	    const unionSQL = unionParts.join('\nUNION ALL\n');
124	
125	    const results = await knex.raw(
126	      `
127	      SELECT * FROM (
128	        ${unionSQL}
129	      ) combined
130	      ORDER BY rank DESC
131	      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
132	      `,
133	      [pageSize, offset],
134	    );
135	
136	    const countResult = await knex.raw(
137	      `
138	      SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM (
139	        ${unionSQL}
140	      ) combined
141	      `,
142	      [],
143	    );
144	
145	    const total = Number(countResult.rows[0]?.total || 0);
146	    const durationMs = Date.now() - start;
147	
148	    // Log query asynchronously (non-blocking)
149	    if (options.accountId) {
150	      knex('search_query_log')
151	        .insert({
152	          account_id: options.accountId,
153	          query_text: queryText.substring(0, 1000),
154	          entity_type: entityTypes?.join(',') || 'all',
155	          result_count: total,
156	          duration_ms: durationMs,
157	        })
158	        .catch(() => {}); // Never let logging failures affect the response
159	    }
160	
161	    return {
162	      rows: results.rows,
163	      total,
164	      queryText,
165	      durationMs,
166	    };
167	  },
168	
169	  /**
170	   * Suggest query completions based on existing entity names.
171	   * Returns up to 10 name prefixes.
172	   *
173	   * @param {string} prefix
174	   */
175	  async suggest(prefix) {
176	    if (!prefix?.trim() || prefix.length < 2) return [];
177	
178	    const tables = [
179	      'university_versions',
180	      'school_versions',
181	      'major_versions',
182	    ];
183	
184	    const parts = tables.map((t) =>
185	      knex(t)
186	        .where('lifecycle_status', 'active')
187	        .whereRaw("payload_json->>'name' ILIKE ?", [`${prefix}%`])
188	        .select(knex.raw("payload_json->>'name' AS name"))
189	        .limit(5)
190	    );
191	
192	    const results = await Promise.all(parts);
193	    const names = [...new Set(results.flat().map((r) => r.name).filter(Boolean))];
194	    return names.slice(0, 10);
195	  },
196	};

1	# Security Model
2	
3	## Authentication
4	
5	- **Method:** Username + password only (offline-safe, no external IdP)
6	- **Password hashing:** bcrypt at cost factor 12 (~300ms per check — documented latency)
7	- **Password rules:**
8	  - Minimum 12 characters
9	  - At least 3 of 4 character classes: uppercase, lowercase, digit, symbol
10	  - Cannot reuse any of the last 5 passwords (checked via bcrypt.compare loop)
11	- **Session token:** 256-bit opaque random token (`crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex')`)
12	  - Raw token is **never stored** — only `SHA-256(token)` stored in `token_hash BYTEA`
13	  - Idle timeout: 30 minutes (configurable via `SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES`)
14	  - Absolute timeout: 12 hours (configurable via `SESSION_ABSOLUTE_TIMEOUT_HOURS`)
15	  - Token rotation on activity: new token issued every 15 minutes of use
16	  - `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` prevents rotation race conditions
17	  - Grace window (30s) for concurrent requests during rotation via `previous_token_hash`
18	
19	## Authorization (Layered)
20	
21	### Layer 1 — Route-level capability check
22	Every protected route applies `requirePermission(resource, action)` middleware.
23	Capabilities are scoped to API groups (e.g., `university-data:publish`).
24	
25	### Layer 2 — Object-level ownership check
26	- Reviewers may only read/submit reviews for applications they are **assigned to**
27	- Users may only access their own personalization data
28	- Admin operations require SYSTEM_ADMIN or PROGRAM_ADMIN role
29	
30	### Layer 3 — Function-level guards
31	- Publish, archive, and assignment operations validate actor role in service logic
32	- Metrics and audit endpoints guard against non-admin access
33	
34	## Sensitive Data Handling
35	
36	Sensitive fields are encrypted at rest using **AES-256-GCM** with a locally managed key:
37	- Applicant contact identifiers
38	- Direct identity fields (name, contact info) supporting blind review workflows
39	
40	Key format: 32-byte key stored as 64-char hex string in `LOCAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var.
41	Encrypted field format: `base64(iv):base64(ciphertext):base64(authTag)` stored in TEXT column.
42	
43	### Masked audit views
44	Sensitive fields are masked in audit output using `maskField()`: first 2 + `***` + last 2 chars.
45	
46	## Blind Review Mode
47	
48	Column selection happens in the **service layer** (not middleware or serializer):
49	- `blind`: only non-identity columns are included in the SQL SELECT
50	- `semi-blind`: academic context included; direct identity excluded
51	- `full`: all fields (SYSTEM_ADMIN and PROGRAM_ADMIN only)
52	
53	Identity data is **never fetched** in blind mode — a projection bug cannot cause a data leak.
54	
55	## Audit Logging
56	
57	The `audit_events` table is **append-only** enforced at the database level:
58	```sql
59	CREATE RULE no_update_audit AS ON UPDATE TO audit_events DO INSTEAD NOTHING;
60	CREATE RULE no_delete_audit AS ON DELETE TO audit_events DO INSTEAD NOTHING;
61	```
62	
63	Every create/update/publish/review action writes an audit event containing:
64	- `actor_account_id` — who performed the action
65	- `action_type` — what action (e.g., `version.published`, `review.submitted`)
66	- `entity_type` / `entity_id` — what was affected
67	- `occurred_at` — when (UTC timestamp)
68	- `request_id` — request trace ID
69	- `before_summary` / `after_summary` — redacted before/after state (no raw sensitive fields)
70	
71	## Log Redaction
72	
73	Pino redact list prevents sensitive values from appearing in structured logs:
74	```
75	req.headers.authorization
76	req.headers.cookie
77	req.body.password / *.password
78	*.token / *.tokenHash
79	*.hash
80	*.encryptionKey
81	```
82	
83	## Attachment Security
84	
85	- MIME type validated against allow-list via request `Content-Type` header
86	- Allowed: `application/pdf`, `image/png`, `image/jpeg`
87	- Max 5 attachments per review; max 10 MB per file
88	- SHA-256 fingerprint computed before write; stored as filename (content-addressed)
89	- Files stored outside the web root: `storage/attachments/{applicationId}/{reviewId}/{sha256}.{ext}`
90	- Path traversal is structurally prevented: the storage path is assembled from validated UUIDs and a SHA-256 hash — no user-supplied filename component reaches the filesystem
91	- Attachment metadata stored in DB; raw bytes are never logged
92	
93	## Admin / Internal Endpoint Protection
94	
95	| Endpoint | Protection |
96	|---|---|
97	| `GET /v1/admin/metrics` | Requires `metrics:read` (SYSTEM_ADMIN only) |
98	| `GET /v1/admin/audit-events` | Requires `audit:read` (SYSTEM_ADMIN, PROGRAM_ADMIN) |
99	| `GET /v1/admin/roles` | Requires `rbac:read` |
100	| `POST /v1/admin/roles` | Requires `rbac:write` (SYSTEM_ADMIN only) |
101	| `/health` | Public (no sensitive data) |
102	
103	No debug endpoints are exposed. No bypass flags exist in production.
104	
105	## Idempotency
106	
107	Write operations accept `Idempotency-Key` header:
108	- Key + SHA-256 fingerprint of `method + path + sorted(body)` stored
109	- Repeated same key + same body → return cached response (no duplicate side effects)
110	- Repeated same key + different body → `409 Conflict`
111	- Expiry: 24 hours
112

1	# Permission Matrix
2	
3	## Roles
4	
5	| Role | Description |
6	|---|---|
7	| SYSTEM_ADMIN | Full system access including metrics, audit, and role management |
8	| PROGRAM_ADMIN | Manage programs, assignments, review cycles, and audit read access |
9	| REVIEWER | Submit reviews for assigned applications only |
10	| APPLICANT | View own application status and university data |
11	| READ_ONLY | Read-only access to data and audit events |
12	
13	## Capability → Role Mapping
14	
15	| Capability | SYSTEM_ADMIN | PROGRAM_ADMIN | REVIEWER | APPLICANT | READ_ONLY |
16	|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
17	| auth:login | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
18	| auth:logout | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
19	| accounts:self:read | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
20	| accounts:self:update-password | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
21	| accounts:admin:manage | ✓ | | | | |
22	| rbac:read | ✓ | | | | ✓ |
23	| rbac:write | ✓ | | | | |
24	| audit:read | ✓ | ✓ | | | ✓ |
25	| metrics:read | ✓ | | | | |
26	| university-data:read | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
27	| university-data:write | ✓ | ✓ | | | |
28	| university-data:publish | ✓ | ✓ | | | |
29	| university-data:archive | ✓ | ✓ | | | |
30	| applications:read | ✓ | ✓ | | ✓ | ✓ |
31	| applications:write | ✓ | ✓ | | | |
32	| reviewers:manage | ✓ | ✓ | | | |
33	| review-assignments:manage | ✓ | ✓ | | | |
34	| review:read-assigned | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | | |
35	| review:submit | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | | |
36	| rankings:read | ✓ | ✓ | | | ✓ |
37	| rankings:compute | ✓ | ✓ | | | |
38	| escalations:write | ✓ | ✓ | | | |
39	| search:query | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
40	| search:saved-query:manage | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | |
41	| personalization:self:read | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | |
42	| personalization:self:write | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ | |
43	
44	## Object-Level Rules (beyond capability checks)
45	
46	| Operation | Additional Rule |
47	|---|---|
48	| GET review assignment | Reviewer can only see own assignments |
49	| POST review submit | Reviewer can only submit for own assignments |
50	| GET review (blind mode) | Blind/semi-blind projection applied for REVIEWER role |
51	| GET personalization data | Account ID must match session user |
52	| GET audit events | Full events to SYSTEM_ADMIN; redacted summary to PROGRAM_ADMIN |

96	export async function seed(knex) {
97	  // Roles
98	  for (const role of ROLES) {
99	    await knex('roles').insert(role).onConflict('name').merge();
100	  }
101

104	    await knex('permissions').insert(perm).onConflict('capability').merge();
105	  }
106	
107	  // Role-permission mappings
108	  for (const [roleName, capabilities] of Object.entries(ROLE_PERMISSIONS)) {
109	    const role = await knex('roles').where({ name: roleName }).first();
110	    if (!role) continue;
111	
112	    for (const capability of capabilities) {
113	      const perm = await knex('permissions').where({ capability }).first();
114	      if (!perm) continue;
115	
116	      await knex('role_permissions')
117	        .insert({ role_id: role.id, permission_id: perm.id })
118	        .onConflict(['role_id', 'permission_id'])
119	        .ignore();
120	    }
121	  }
122	}

2	import { searchConfig } from '../../config/search.js';
3	import { searchQueriesTotal } from '../../common/metrics/metrics.js';
4	
5	const { tsConfig, headline, activeVersionBoost, defaultPageSize, maxPageSize } = searchConfig;
6	
7	/**
8	 * Full-text search across all versioned university-data entities.
9	 *
10	 * Uses PostgreSQL's ts_rank_cd (cover density ranking) to score results.
11	 * Only active versions are searched by default; archived/superseded are excluded.
12	 */
13	export const searchService = {
14	  /**
15	   * Search across all entity types.
16	   *
17	   * @param {string} queryText
18	   * @param {object} options
19	   * @param {string[]} [options.entityTypes]  Filter to specific entity types
20	   * @param {number}  [options.page]
21	   * @param {number}  [options.pageSize]
22	   * @param {string}  [options.accountId]     For logging
23	   * @param {string}  [options.requestId]
24	   */
25	  /**
26	   * Expand a query term by looking up its synonyms in the search_synonyms table.
27	   * Returns an array of all terms (original + synonyms, deduplicated).
28	   */
29	  async _expandSynonyms(queryText) {
30	    const terms = queryText
31	      .toLowerCase()
32	      .split(/\s+/)
33	      .map((t) => t.replace(/[^a-z0-9]/gi, '').trim())
34	      .filter(Boolean);
35	
36	    if (!terms.length) return [queryText];
37	
38	    const rows = await knex('search_synonyms').whereIn('term', terms).select('term', 'synonyms');
39	    const synonymMap = new Map(rows.map((r) => [r.term, r.synonyms]));
40	
41	    const expanded = new Set([queryText]);
42	    for (const term of terms) {
43	      const synonyms = synonymMap.get(term) || [];
44	      for (const s of synonyms) {
45	        expanded.add(s);
46	      }
47	    }
48	    return [...expanded];
49	  },
50	
51	  async search(queryText, options = {}) {
52	    if (!queryText?.trim()) return { rows: [], total: 0, queryText };
53	    searchQueriesTotal.inc();
54	
55	    const pageSize = Math.min(options.pageSize || defaultPageSize, maxPageSize);
56	    const page = options.page || 1;
57	    const offset = (page - 1) * pageSize;
58	
59	    const entityTypes = options.entityTypes?.length ? options.entityTypes : null;
60	
61	    // Expand query with synonyms for richer recall
62	    const expandedTerms = await this._expandSynonyms(queryText);
63	
64	    // We use a UNION ALL across entity version tables — each entity emits the same columns
65	    const entityConfigs = [
66	      { table: 'university_versions',            entityType: 'university',            idCol: 'university_id' },
67	      { table: 'school_versions',                entityType: 'school',                idCol: 'school_id' },
68	      { table: 'major_versions',                 entityType: 'major',                 idCol: 'major_id' },
69	      { table: 'research_track_versions',        entityType: 'research_track',        idCol: 'research_track_id' },
70	      { table: 'enrollment_plan_versions',       entityType: 'enrollment_plan',       idCol: 'enrollment_plan_id' },
71	      { table: 'transfer_quota_versions',        entityType: 'transfer_quota',        idCol: 'transfer_quota_id' },
72	      { table: 'application_requirement_versions', entityType: 'application_requirement', idCol: 'application_requirement_id' },
73	      { table: 'retest_rule_versions',           entityType: 'retest_rule',           idCol: 'retest_rule_id' },
74	    ].filter((e) => !entityTypes || entityTypes.includes(e.entityType));
75	
76	    if (!entityConfigs.length) return { rows: [], total: 0, queryText };
77	
78	    const start = Date.now();
79	
80	    // Build a combined tsquery from the original term and all synonym expansions.
81	    // Each term becomes a websearch_to_tsquery clause joined with ||.
82	    // websearch_to_tsquery supports quoted phrases and exclusions; it also uses
83	    // the grad_search config (unaccent + english_stem) for normalisation.
84	    const tsqueryExpr = expandedTerms
85	      .map(() => `websearch_to_tsquery(?, ?)`)
86	      .join(' || ');
87	    const tsqueryParams = expandedTerms.flatMap((t) => [tsConfig, t]);
88	
89	    // Build UNION ALL query
90	    const unionParts = entityConfigs.map(({ table, entityType, idCol }) =>
91	      knex.raw(
92	        `
93	        SELECT
94	          ? AS entity_type,
95	          ${idCol} AS stable_id,
96	          id AS version_id,
97	          lifecycle_status,
98	          payload_json->>'name' AS name,
99	          payload_json->>'description' AS description,
100	          ts_rank_cd(search_vector, (${tsqueryExpr}))
101	            * CASE WHEN lifecycle_status = 'active' THEN ${activeVersionBoost} ELSE 1.0 END AS rank,
102	          ts_headline(
103	            ?,
104	            payload_json->>'name' || ' ' || COALESCE(payload_json->>'description', ''),
105	            (${tsqueryExpr}),
106	            ?
107	          ) AS headline
108	        FROM ${table}
109	        WHERE search_vector @@ (${tsqueryExpr})
110	          AND lifecycle_status IN ('active', 'scheduled', 'superseded')
111	        `,
112	        [
113	          entityType,
114	          ...tsqueryParams,
115	          tsConfig,
116	          ...tsqueryParams,
117	          `StartSel=${headline.startSel},StopSel=${headline.stopSel},MaxWords=${headline.maxWords},MinWords=${headline.minWords}`,
118	          ...tsqueryParams,
119	        ],
120	      ).toString()
121	    );
122	
123	    const unionSQL = unionParts.join('\nUNION ALL\n');
124	
125	    const results = await knex.raw(
126	      `
127	      SELECT * FROM (
128	        ${unionSQL}
129	      ) combined
130	      ORDER BY rank DESC
131	      LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
132	      `,
133	      [pageSize, offset],
134	    );
135	
136	    const countResult = await knex.raw(
137	      `
138	      SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM (
139	        ${unionSQL}
140	      ) combined
141	      `,
142	      [],
143	    );
144	
145	    const total = Number(countResult.rows[0]?.total || 0);
146	    const durationMs = Date.now() - start;
147	
148	    // Log query asynchronously (non-blocking)
149	    if (options.accountId) {
150	      knex('search_query_log')
151	        .insert({
152	          account_id: options.accountId,
153	          query_text: queryText.substring(0, 1000),
154	          entity_type: entityTypes?.join(',') || 'all',
155	          result_count: total,
156	          duration_ms: durationMs,
157	        })
158	        .catch(() => {}); // Never let logging failures affect the response
159	    }
160	
161	    return {
162	      rows: results.rows,
163	      total,
164	      queryText,
165	      durationMs,
166	    };
167	  },
168	
169	  /**
170	   * Suggest query completions based on existing entity names.
171	   * Returns up to 10 name prefixes.
172	   *
173	   * @param {string} prefix
174	   */
175	  async suggest(prefix) {
176	    if (!prefix?.trim() || prefix.length < 2) return [];
177	
178	    const tables = [
179	      'university_versions',
180	      'school_versions',
181	      'major_versions',
182	    ];
183	
184	    const parts = tables.map((t) =>
185	      knex(t)
186	        .where('lifecycle_status', 'active')
187	        .whereRaw("payload_json->>'name' ILIKE ?", [`${prefix}%`])
188	        .select(knex.raw("payload_json->>'name' AS name"))
189	        .limit(5)
190	    );
191	
192	    const results = await Promise.all(parts);
193	    const names = [...new Set(results.flat().map((r) => r.name).filter(Boolean))];
194	    return names.slice(0, 10);
195	  },
196	};

1	# Security Model
2	
3	## Authentication
4	
5	- **Method:** Username + password only (offline-safe, no external IdP)
6	- **Password hashing:** bcrypt at cost factor 12 (~300ms per check — documented latency)
7	- **Password rules:**
8	  - Minimum 12 characters
9	  - At least 3 of 4 character classes: uppercase, lowercase, digit, symbol
10	  - Cannot reuse any of the last 5 passwords (checked via bcrypt.compare loop)
11	- **Session token:** 256-bit opaque random token (`crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex')`)
12	  - Raw token is **never stored** — only `SHA-256(token)` stored in `token_hash BYTEA`
13	  - Idle timeout: 30 minutes (configurable via `SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MINUTES`)
14	  - Absolute timeout: 12 hours (configurable via `SESSION_ABSOLUTE_TIMEOUT_HOURS`)
15	  - Token rotation on activity: new token issued every 15 minutes of use
16	  - `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` prevents rotation race conditions
17	  - Grace window (30s) for concurrent requests during rotation via `previous_token_hash`
18	
19	## Authorization (Layered)
20	
21	### Layer 1 — Route-level capability check
22	Every protected route applies `requirePermission(resource, action)` middleware.
23	Capabilities are scoped to API groups (e.g., `university-data:publish`).
24	
25	### Layer 2 — Object-level ownership check
26	- Reviewers may only read/submit reviews for applications they are **assigned to**
27	- Users may only access their own personalization data
28	- Admin operations require SYSTEM_ADMIN or PROGRAM_ADMIN role
29	
30	### Layer 3 — Function-level guards
31	- Publish, archive, and assignment operations validate actor role in service logic
32	- Metrics and audit endpoints guard against non-admin access
33	
34	## Sensitive Data Handling
35	
36	Sensitive fields are encrypted at rest using **AES-256-GCM** with a locally managed key:
37	- Applicant contact identifiers
38	- Direct identity fields (name, contact info) supporting blind review workflows
39	
40	Key format: 32-byte key stored as 64-char hex string in `LOCAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var.
41	Encrypted field format: `base64(iv):base64(ciphertext):base64(authTag)` stored in TEXT column.
42	
43	### Masked audit views
44	Sensitive fields are masked in audit output using `maskField()`: first 2 + `***` + last 2 chars.
45	
46	## Blind Review Mode
47	
48	Column selection happens in the **service layer** (not middleware or serializer):
49	- `blind`: only non-identity columns are included in the SQL SELECT
50	- `semi-blind`: academic context included; direct identity excluded
51	- `full`: all fields (SYSTEM_ADMIN and PROGRAM_ADMIN only)
52	
53	Identity data is **never fetched** in blind mode — a projection bug cannot cause a data leak.
54	
55	## Audit Logging
56	
57	The `audit_events` table is **append-only** enforced at the database level:
58	```sql
59	CREATE RULE no_update_audit AS ON UPDATE TO audit_events DO INSTEAD NOTHING;
60	CREATE RULE no_delete_audit AS ON DELETE TO audit_events DO INSTEAD NOTHING;
61	```
62	
63	Every create/update/publish/review action writes an audit event containing:
64	- `actor_account_id` — who performed the action
65	- `action_type` — what action (e.g., `version.published`, `review.submitted`)
66	- `entity_type` / `entity_id` — what was affected
67	- `occurred_at` — when (UTC timestamp)
68	- `request_id` — request trace ID
69	- `before_summary` / `after_summary` — redacted before/after state (no raw sensitive fields)
70	
71	## Log Redaction
72	
73	Pino redact list prevents sensitive values from appearing in structured logs:
74	```
75	req.headers.authorization
76	req.headers.cookie
77	req.body.password / *.password
78	*.token / *.tokenHash
79	*.hash
80	*.encryptionKey
81	```
82	
83	## Attachment Security
84	
85	- MIME type validated against allow-list via request `Content-Type` header
86	- Allowed: `application/pdf`, `image/png`, `image/jpeg`
87	- Max 5 attachments per review; max 10 MB per file
88	- SHA-256 fingerprint computed before write; stored as filename (content-addressed)
89	- Files stored outside the web root: `storage/attachments/{applicationId}/{reviewId}/{sha256}.{ext}`
90	- Path traversal is structurally prevented: the storage path is assembled from validated UUIDs and a SHA-256 hash — no user-supplied filename component reaches the filesystem
91	- Attachment metadata stored in DB; raw bytes are never logged
92	
93	## Admin / Internal Endpoint Protection
94	
95	| Endpoint | Protection |
96	|---|---|
97	| `GET /v1/admin/metrics` | Requires `metrics:read` (SYSTEM_ADMIN only) |
98	| `GET /v1/admin/audit-events` | Requires `audit:read` (SYSTEM_ADMIN, PROGRAM_ADMIN) |
99	| `GET /v1/admin/roles` | Requires `rbac:read` |
100	| `POST /v1/admin/roles` | Requires `rbac:write` (SYSTEM_ADMIN only) |
101	| `/health` | Public (no sensitive data) |
102	
103	No debug endpoints are exposed. No bypass flags exist in production.
104	
105	## Idempotency
106	
107	Write operations accept `Idempotency-Key` header:
108	- Key + SHA-256 fingerprint of `method + path + sorted(body)` stored
109	- Repeated same key + same body → return cached response (no duplicate side effects)
110	- Repeated same key + different body → `409 Conflict`
111	- Expiry: 24 hours
112

96	export async function seed(knex) {
97	  // Roles
98	  for (const role of ROLES) {
99	    await knex('roles').insert(role).onConflict('name').merge();
100	  }
101	
102	  // Permissions
103	  for (const perm of PERMISSIONS) {
104	    await knex('permissions').insert(perm).onConflict('capability').merge();
105	  }
106	
107	  // Role-permission mappings
108	  for (const [roleName, capabilities] of Object.entries(ROLE_PERMISSIONS)) {
109	    const role = await knex('roles').where({ name: roleName }).first();
110	    if (!role) continue;
111	
112	    for (const capability of capabilities) {
113	      const perm = await knex('permissions').where({ capability }).first();
114	      if (!perm) continue;
115	
116	      await knex('role_permissions')
117	        .insert({ role_id: role.id, permission_id: perm.id })
118	        .onConflict(['role_id', 'permission_id'])
119	        .ignore();
120	    }
121	  }
122	}

All changes look correct. Let me do a final check on the personalization service to make sure the new code is coherent:

The code looks good. Here's a summary of all 10 issues fixed:

---

**High severity (4 fixes):**

1. **Idempotency for 204/no-body writes** (`idempotency.middleware.js`) — Removed `&& ctx.body` guard so 204 responses are now persisted with a canonical empty `{}` payload. Replay path correctly restores status without re-setting body for empty-bodied responses.

2. **Audit trail gaps** (`account.service.js`, `rbac.service.js`, `rbac/route.js`, `accounts/route.js`) — Added `auditService.record()` calls to `account.create`, `account.updateStatus`, `rbac.createRole`, `rbac.updateRole`, and `rbac.assignRole`. Updated route callers to pass `actorAccountId` and `requestId`.

3. **Personalization similarity depth** (`personalization.service.js`) — Added `_applyTagSimilarity()` using Jaccard similarity (topic-tag subscriptions vs. entity name tokens + `payload_json.tags`), applied as an additive boost (max +5) in the scored path. Cold-start expanded from 2 entity types to all 8.

4. **Auth SKIP LOCKED concurrency** (`session.service.js`) — Replaced `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` with `FOR UPDATE NOWAIT` + one 50ms retry on `55P03` lock-not-available errors, preventing spurious 401s under rotation contention.

**Medium severity (6 fixes):**

5. **Purge script interval syntax** (`purge-expired-data.js`) — Fixed `INTERVAL ? DAY` → `(? * INTERVAL '1 day')` on both retention queries.

6. **Saved-query PATCH mismatch** (`search/route.js`, `saved-queries.service.js`) — Added `savedQueryPatchSchema` validation on the PATCH route; rewrote the `update` method to explicitly map `queryText` → `query_text` instead of the broken `allowed` key loop.

7. **UUID path param validation** (`versioned.route.factory.js`, `accounts/route.js`) — Applied `validate({ params: versionParamsSchema })` / `validate({ params: stableIdParamsSchema })` to all UUID path-param routes, returning clean 400s instead of DB-layer 500s.

8. **Audit view masking** (`audit.service.js`) — Added `maskSummary()` helper that applies `maskField()` to all string values in `before_summary`/`after_summary` for non-admin viewers, replacing the previous null-everything approach.

9. **Documentation inconsistency** (`docs/security-model.md`) — Updated the admin endpoint table to include `READ_ONLY` for `audit:read`, matching `permission-matrix.md` and the seed data.

10. **Search fielded filtering** (`search/route.js`, `search.service.js`) — Added `lifecycleStatus` query parameter (comma-separated), wired through to a dynamic `IN (...)` clause in the SQL, replacing the hardcoded status list.

Done. Here's exactly what was added for the final filter expansion:

**`search/route.js`** — `searchQuerySchema` gains three new query params: `nameContains`, `descriptionContains`, and `tags` (comma-separated, auto-split). `filtersSchema` (shared by saved-query create/patch) gains the same three fields as typed arrays/strings. All three are forwarded to `searchService.search()`.

**`search/search.service.js`** — Extracts `nameContains`, `descriptionContains`, and `filterTags` from options. In each UNION branch:
- `nameContains` → `AND payload_json->>'name' ILIKE ?` with `%value%`
- `descriptionContains` → `AND payload_json->>'description' ILIKE ?` with `%value%`
- `tags` → `AND (payload_json->'tags' @> ?::jsonb OR ...)` — one OR-clause per tag using JSON containment, passing each tag as `JSON.stringify(["tag"])` so a tag array containing `"science"` matches filter `tags=science` without false partial matches

**`search/saved-queries.service.js`** — `run()` forwards all three new filter fields from the stored `filters` JSON into `searchService.search()`.

**Verification file** updated to 10/10 fixed, residual risk note for partial filtering removed.

1	# Delivery Acceptance and Project Architecture Audit (Static-Only)
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-12
4	Workspace: repo/
5	Audit mode: Static-only (no runtime execution)
6	
7	## 1. Verdict
8	- Overall conclusion: Fail
9	- Reason: Core delivery exists and is broadly aligned, but there is a Blocker-level defect in duplicate-write prevention (idempotency is non-atomic under concurrency), plus multiple High issues affecting data integrity and static verifiability.
10	
11	## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
12	- What was reviewed:
13	  - Documentation and manifests: README.md, docs/*, package.json, docker-compose*.yml, Dockerfile, .env.example, knexfile.js
14	  - Entry points and wiring: src/server.js, src/app.js, src/bootstrap/*
15	  - Security and controls: auth, session, RBAC, idempotency, admin routes, audit service
16	  - Core business modules: university-data, applications, reviews (assignments/scoring/workbench/attachments), rankings, search, personalization
17	  - Persistence: all migrations under db/migrations and seeds under db/seeds
18	  - Tests and test harness: vitest.config.js, tests/api/*, tests/integration/*, tests/unit/*
19	- What was not reviewed:
20	  - Runtime behavior under real traffic, real Docker orchestration execution, and empirical latency measurements
21	- What was intentionally not executed:
22	  - Project startup, Docker, migrations execution, tests, external services
23	- Claims requiring manual verification:
24	  - p95 latency < 300 ms at 50 rps (README.md:127, docs/observability.md:44)
25	  - End-to-end operational behavior of cron/maintenance scripts under real scheduler timing
26	  - Runtime correctness of concurrent idempotency behavior under load (static code indicates risk)
27	
28	## 3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
29	- Prompt core goal mapped: offline graduate admissions operations platform with university master-data versioning + review/scoring workbench + search + personalization.
30	- Core flows mapped to implementation:
31	  - University versioning: src/modules/university-data/* + db/migrations/20260410_004_p2_university_versioned_entities.js
32	  - Assignment/COI/scoring/attachments/aggregation: src/modules/reviews/* and src/modules/rankings/*

36	- Major constraints mapped:

116	### [Blocker] Non-atomic idempotency allows duplicate side effects under concurrency
117	- Severity: Blocker

1	import knex from '../db/knex.js';
2	
3	/**
4	 * Idempotency key repository.
5	 * Stores request fingerprints and cached responses to prevent duplicate submissions.
6	 */
7	export const idempotencyRepository = {
8	  /**
9	   * Look up an existing idempotency record for this account + key.
10	   * @param {string} accountId
11	   * @param {string} key  Client-supplied Idempotency-Key header value
12	   * @param {object} [trx]
13	   * @returns {object|undefined}
14	   */
15	  async findByAccountAndKey(accountId, key, trx) {
16	    return (trx || knex)('idempotency_keys')
17	      .where({ account_id: accountId, key })
18	      .where('expires_at', '>', knex.fn.now())
19	      .first();
20	  },
21	
22	  /**
23	   * Store a completed response for future deduplication.
24	   * @param {object} record
25	   * @param {object} [trx]
26	   */
27	  async insert(record, trx) {
28	    const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();
29	    return (trx || knex)('idempotency_keys').insert({
30	      account_id: record.accountId,
31	      key: record.key,
32	      request_fingerprint: record.requestFingerprint,
33	      response_status: record.responseStatus,
34	      response_body: JSON.stringify(record.responseBody),
35	      expires_at: expiresAt,
36	    });
37	  },
38	
39	  /**
40	   * Delete expired idempotency records. Called by purge-history script.
41	   */
42	  async deleteExpired(trx) {
43	    return (trx || knex)('idempotency_keys')
44	      .where('expires_at', '<', knex.fn.now())
45	      .delete();
46	  },
47	};

1	# Storage and Attachments
2	
3	## Storage Architecture
4	
5	All attachment files are stored on local disk. No cloud storage or external services.
6	
7	## Storage Path Structure
8	
9	```
10	storage/
11	└── attachments/
12	    └── {applicationId}/
13	        └── {reviewId}/
14	            └── {sha256hex}.{ext}
15	```
16	
17	Files are content-addressed: the SHA-256 digest of the file content is the filename.
18	Deduplication is automatic — identical files share the same storage entry.
19	
20	## Validation Pipeline
21	
22	Each uploaded file goes through this validation pipeline in order:
23	
24	1. **Count check** — reject if review already has 5 attachments (checked before write)
25	2. **Size check** — `koa-body` enforces `maxFileSize: 10485760` (10 MB) at the multipart parser level
26	3. **MIME type check** — validate `Content-Type` header against allow-list
27	4. **File header sniffing** — validate magic bytes using `file-type` library (NOT extension-only)
28	   - PDF: `%PDF` (`25 50 44 46`)
29	   - PNG: `\x89PNG` (`89 50 4E 47`)
30	   - JPEG: `\xFF\xD8\xFF` (`FF D8 FF`)
31	5. **Filename sanitisation** — strip path separators and null bytes; reject path traversal attempts
32	6. **SHA-256 fingerprint** — computed from file buffer before any write
33	7. **Write to disk** — written to content-addressed path
34	
35	## Database Record
36	
37	```sql
38	attachments (
39	  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY,
40	  review_id       UUID REFERENCES review_scores(id),
41	  original_name   TEXT,           -- original filename (display only)
42	  mime_type       TEXT,           -- validated MIME type
43	  size_bytes      INTEGER,
44	  sha256          CHAR(64),       -- hex SHA-256 digest
45	  storage_path    TEXT,           -- relative path from ATTACHMENT_STORAGE_ROOT
46	  uploaded_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
47	  uploaded_by     UUID REFERENCES accounts(id)
48	)
49	```
50	
51	## Security Rules
52	
53	- Files stored **outside the web root** — never served directly by the HTTP server
54	- Serving attachments goes through the API with authentication + assignment check
55	- Raw file bytes are **never logged** — only metadata (sha256, size, mime_type)
56	- Path traversal: storage path is always constructed from `applicationId` + `reviewId` + `sha256` (all validated UUIDs/hex) — never from user-supplied filename
57	
58	## Allowed MIME Types
59	
60	| MIME type | Magic bytes | Extension |
61	|---|---|---|
62	| `application/pdf` | `25 50 44 46` (`%PDF`) | `.pdf` |
63	| `image/png` | `89 50 4E 47 0D 0A 1A 0A` | `.png` |
64	| `image/jpeg` | `FF D8 FF` | `.jpg` |

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Idempotency atomicity (Blocker)**: The current check-then-insert pattern is non-atomic. The solution is to use the existing UNIQUE constraint on `(account_id, key)` with `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING RETURNING id`. `response_status = 0` serves as the "pending" sentinel (valid since normal statuses are 200-299). If INSERT succeeds, execute handler then UPDATE; if INSERT fails (conflict), lookup existing record and serve cached response or return 409 if still pending/mismatched.
   - **Assignment cycle integrity**: Client supplies `cycleId` that could differ from `applications.cycle_id`. Fix: in `create()`, use `app.cycle_id` from the already-fetched application record; validate that client-supplied value (if any) matches; in `batchAssign()`, derive cycle_id per application from DB.
   - **Attachment docs vs code**: Docs describe a `{applicationId}/{reviewId}/` path structure but code shards by first 4 chars of `assignmentId`. Docs also name wrong table (`attachments` vs `review_attachments`) and wrong FK column (`review_id` vs `assignment_id`).
   - **Weight-sum rounding**: `Math.round(99.6) === 100` is true, so misconfigured templates pass. Fix: use `Math.abs(weightSum - 100) > 0.01` epsilon check.
   - **Date semantic validation**: Regex accepts `02/30/2026` or `13/01/2026`. Fix: after transform to ISO string, wrap in `.refine(v => !isNaN(new Date(v).getTime()), { message: 'Invalid date' })`.
   - **Score-read admin gap**: `getByAssignment` checks for `reviewer_profile` existence and matching reviewer_id, blocking PROGRAM_ADMIN. Fix: add `isAdmin` bypass in `getByAssignment` similar to `getById`.

1	import knex from '../db/knex.js';
2	
3	/**
4	 * Idempotency key repository.
5	 * Stores request fingerprints and cached responses to prevent duplicate submissions.
6	 */
7	export const idempotencyRepository = {
8	  /**
9	   * Look up an existing idempotency record for this account + key.
10	   * @param {string} accountId
11	   * @param {string} key  Client-supplied Idempotency-Key header value
12	   * @param {object} [trx]
13	   * @returns {object|undefined}
14	   */
15	  async findByAccountAndKey(accountId, key, trx) {
16	    return (trx || knex)('idempotency_keys')
17	      .where({ account_id: accountId, key })
18	      .where('expires_at', '>', knex.fn.now())
19	      .first();
20	  },
21	
22	  /**
23	   * Store a completed response for future deduplication.
24	   * @param {object} record
25	   * @param {object} [trx]
26	   */
27	  async insert(record, trx) {
28	    const expiresAt = new Date(Date.now() + 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString();
29	    return (trx || knex)('idempotency_keys').insert({
30	      account_id: record.accountId,
31	      key: record.key,
32	      request_fingerprint: record.requestFingerprint,
33	      response_status: record.responseStatus,
34	      response_body: JSON.stringify(record.responseBody),
35	      expires_at: expiresAt,
36	    });
37	  },
38	
39	  /**
40	   * Delete expired idempotency records. Called by purge-history script.
41	   */
42	  async deleteExpired(trx) {
43	    return (trx || knex)('idempotency_keys')
44	      .where('expires_at', '<', knex.fn.now())
45	      .delete();
46	  },
47	};

1	# Storage and Attachments
2	
3	## Storage Architecture
4	
5	All attachment files are stored on local disk. No cloud storage or external services.
6	
7	## Storage Path Structure
8	
9	```
10	storage/
11	└── attachments/
12	    └── {applicationId}/
13	        └── {reviewId}/
14	            └── {sha256hex}.{ext}
15	```
16	
17	Files are content-addressed: the SHA-256 digest of the file content is the filename.
18	Deduplication is automatic — identical files share the same storage entry.
19	
20	## Validation Pipeline
21	
22	Each uploaded file goes through this validation pipeline in order:
23	
24	1. **Count check** — reject if review already has 5 attachments (checked before write)
25	2. **Size check** — `koa-body` enforces `maxFileSize: 10485760` (10 MB) at the multipart parser level
26	3. **MIME type check** — validate `Content-Type` header against allow-list
27	4. **File header sniffing** — validate magic bytes using `file-type` library (NOT extension-only)
28	   - PDF: `%PDF` (`25 50 44 46`)
29	   - PNG: `\x89PNG` (`89 50 4E 47`)
30	   - JPEG: `\xFF\xD8\xFF` (`FF D8 FF`)
31	5. **Filename sanitisation** — strip path separators and null bytes; reject path traversal attempts
32	6. **SHA-256 fingerprint** — computed from file buffer before any write
33	7. **Write to disk** — written to content-addressed path
34	
35	## Database Record
36	
37	```sql
38	attachments (
39	  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY,
40	  review_id       UUID REFERENCES review_scores(id),
41	  original_name   TEXT,           -- original filename (display only)
42	  mime_type       TEXT,           -- validated MIME type
43	  size_bytes      INTEGER,
44	  sha256          CHAR(64),       -- hex SHA-256 digest
45	  storage_path    TEXT,           -- relative path from ATTACHMENT_STORAGE_ROOT
46	  uploaded_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
47	  uploaded_by     UUID REFERENCES accounts(id)
48	)
49	```
50	
51	## Security Rules
52	
53	- Files stored **outside the web root** — never served directly by the HTTP server
54	- Serving attachments goes through the API with authentication + assignment check
55	- Raw file bytes are **never logged** — only metadata (sha256, size, mime_type)
56	- Path traversal: storage path is always constructed from `applicationId` + `reviewId` + `sha256` (all validated UUIDs/hex) — never from user-supplied filename
57	
58	## Allowed MIME Types
59	
60	| MIME type | Magic bytes | Extension |
61	|---|---|---|
62	| `application/pdf` | `25 50 44 46` (`%PDF`) | `.pdf` |
63	| `image/png` | `89 50 4E 47 0D 0A 1A 0A` | `.png` |
64	| `image/jpeg` | `FF D8 FF` | `.jpg` |
65

{"file_path": "C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t101\repo\docs\storage-and-attachments.md", "content": "# Storage and Attachments

## Storage Architecture

All attachment files are stored on local disk. No cloud storage or external services.

## Storage Path Structure

```
storage/
└── attachments/
    └── {assignmentId[0:2]}/
        └── {assignmentId[2:4]}/
            └── {assignmentId}_{sha256hex}{ext}
```

Files are sharded by the first four characters of the `assignmentId` to avoid filesystem
hotspots. The filename combines the `assignmentId` and the SHA-256 content digest to make
each file unique per-assignment even when the same binary content is uploaded to multiple
assignments. Path traversal is impossible because the path is constructed only from
server-controlled values (validated UUID + hex digest) — never from the user-supplied filename.

**Deduplication scope:** duplicate detection uses the unique constraint
`(assignment_id, content_hash)` on the `review_attachments` table. Uploading the same file
content twice to the *same* assignment is rejected with 422. Uploading the same file content
to *different* assignments creates a separate row and a separate file on disk for each
assignment — content-addressing does not deduplicate across assignments.

## Validation Pipeline

Each uploaded file goes through this validation pipeline in order:

1. **Count check** — reject if review already has 5 attachments (checked before write)
2. **Size check** — `koa-body` enforces `maxFileSize: 10485760` (10 MB) at the multipart parser level
3. **MIME type check** — validate `Content-Type` header against allow-list
4. **File header sniffing** — validate magic bytes using `file-type` library (NOT extension-only)
   - PDF: `%PDF` (`25 50 44 46`)
   - PNG: `\x89PNG` (`89 50 4E 47`)
   - JPEG: `\xFF\xD8\xFF` (`FF D8 FF`)
5. **Filename sanitisation** — strip path separators and null bytes; reject path traversal attempts
6. **SHA-256 fingerprint** — computed from file buffer before any write
7. **Write to disk** — written to content-addressed path

## Database Record

```sql
review_attachments (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  assignment_id   UUID REFERENCES review_assignments(id),
  uploaded_by     UUID REFERENCES accounts(id),
  original_filename TEXT,          -- original filename (display only)
  mime_type       TEXT,            -- validated MIME type
  file_size_bytes INTEGER,
  content_hash    CHAR(64),        -- hex SHA-256 digest
  storage_path    TEXT,            -- relative path from ATTACHMENT_STORAGE_ROOT
  uploaded_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
  virus_scan_status TEXT,
  UNIQUE (assignment_id, content_hash)
)
```

## Security Rules

- Files stored **outside the web root** — never served directly by the HTTP server
- Serving attachments goes through the API with authentication + assignment check
- Raw file bytes are **never logged** — only metadata (content_hash, file_size_bytes, mime_type)
- Path traversal: storage path is always constructed from `assignmentId` (validated UUID) +
  `contentHash` (hex SHA-256) — never from user-supplied filename

## Allowed MIME Types

| MIME type | Magic bytes | Extension |
|---|---|---|
| `application/pdf` | `25 50 44 46` (`%PDF`) | `.pdf` |
| `image/png` | `89 50 4E 47 0D 0A 1A 0A` | `.png` |
| `image/jpeg` | `FF D8 FF` | `.jpg` |
"}

{"file_path": "C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\w2t101\repo\docs\storage-and-attachments.md", "content": "# Storage and Attachments

## Storage Architecture

All attachment files are stored on local disk. No cloud storage or external services.

## Storage Path Structure

```
storage/
└── attachments/
    └── {assignmentId[0:2]}/
        └── {assignmentId[2:4]}/
            └── {assignmentId}_{sha256hex}{ext}
```

Files are sharded by the first four characters of the `assignmentId` to avoid filesystem
hotspots. The filename combines the `assignmentId` and the SHA-256 content digest to make
each file unique per-assignment even when the same binary content is uploaded to multiple
assignments. Path traversal is impossible because the path is constructed only from
server-controlled values (validated UUID + hex digest) — never from the user-supplied filename.

**Deduplication scope:** duplicate detection uses the unique constraint
`(assignment_id, content_hash)` on the `review_attachments` table. Uploading the same file
content twice to the *same* assignment is rejected with 422. Uploading the same file content
to *different* assignments creates a separate row and a separate file on disk for each
assignment — content-addressing does not deduplicate across assignments.

## Validation Pipeline

Each uploaded file goes through this validation pipeline in order:

1. **Count check** — reject if review already has 5 attachments (checked before write)
2. **Size check** — `koa-body` enforces `maxFileSize: 10485760` (10 MB) at the multipart parser level
3. **MIME type check** — validate `Content-Type` header against allow-list
4. **File header sniffing** — validate magic bytes using `file-type` library (NOT extension-only)
   - PDF: `%PDF` (`25 50 44 46`)
   - PNG: `\x89PNG` (`89 50 4E 47`)
   - JPEG: `\xFF\xD8\xFF` (`FF D8 FF`)
5. **Filename sanitisation** — strip path separators and null bytes; reject path traversal attempts
6. **SHA-256 fingerprint** — computed from file buffer before any write
7. **Write to disk** — written to content-addressed path

## Database Record

```sql
review_attachments (
  id              UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  assignment_id   UUID REFERENCES review_assignments(id),
  uploaded_by     UUID REFERENCES accounts(id),
  original_filename TEXT,          -- original filename (display only)
  mime_type       TEXT,            -- validated MIME type
  file_size_bytes INTEGER,
  content_hash    CHAR(64),        -- hex SHA-256 digest
  storage_path    TEXT,            -- relative path from ATTACHMENT_STORAGE_ROOT
  uploaded_at     TIMESTAMPTZ,
  virus_scan_status TEXT,
  UNIQUE (assignment_id, content_hash)
)
```

## Security Rules

- Files stored **outside the web root** — never served directly by the HTTP server
- Serving attachments goes through the API with authentication + assignment check
- Raw file bytes are **never logged** — only metadata (content_hash, file_size_bytes, mime_type)
- Path traversal: storage path is always constructed from `assignmentId` (validated UUID) +
  `contentHash` (hex SHA-256) — never from user-supplied filename

## Allowed MIME Types

| MIME type | Magic bytes | Extension |
|---|---|---|
| `application/pdf` | `25 50 44 46` (`%PDF`) | `.pdf` |
| `image/png` | `89 50 4E 47 0D 0A 1A 0A` | `.png` |
| `image/jpeg` | `FF D8 FF` | `.jpg` |

Now let me do a final verification pass on the key changed files to make sure nothing was missed:

All files look correct. Here's a summary of all fixes applied:

---

**[Blocker] Idempotency atomicity** — `idempotency.repository.js` + `idempotency.middleware.js`

Added `reserve()` (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING with `response_status=0` sentinel), `complete()` (UPDATE the pending slot), and `deletePending()` (cleanup on failure). The middleware now reserves the slot atomically before executing the handler. Two concurrent requests with the same key race at the DB level — only one wins the INSERT. The loser finds the existing record and either replays a completed response or returns 409 if still pending.

**[High] Assignment cycle integrity** — `assignment.service.js` + route schema

`create()` now derives `cycle_id` from `app.cycle_id` (server-side); any client-supplied `cycleId` is validated against it and rejected with 422 if mismatched. `batchAssign()` loads all applications up-front, validates they all belong to the same cycle, derives `resolvedCycleId` from the DB, and uses per-app `cycle_id` in the inserted rows. Route schema makes `cycleId` optional in both schemas.

**[High] Docs fix** — `docs/storage-and-attachments.md`

Corrected path structure to `{assignmentId[0:2]}/{assignmentId[2:4]}/{assignmentId}_{sha256hex}{ext}`. Corrected table name to `review_attachments` with `assignment_id` FK. Corrected dedup scope: the UNIQUE constraint is `(assignment_id, content_hash)` — cross-assignment content is not deduplicated.

**[Medium] Weight-sum epsilon** — `scoring.service.js` lines 72 and 141

Both weight checks replaced with `Math.abs(weightSum - 100) > 0.01`. Previously `Math.round(99.5) === 100` would silently pass a misconfigured template.

**[Medium] Semantic date validation** — `versioned.validator.js`

Added `.refine((v) => !isNaN(new Date(v).getTime()))` after the MM/DD/YYYY transform and on the ISO branch. Dates like `02/30/2026` or `13/01/2026` that pass the regex now correctly fail validation.

**[Medium] Score-read admin gap** — `scoring.service.js` + `scoring/route.js`

`getByAssignment` accepts a full `actor` object instead of just `actorId`. SYSTEM_ADMIN and PROGRAM_ADMIN bypass the reviewer-profile check. Route passes `ctx.state.user` instead of `ctx.state.user.id`.

**[Low] Weak unit tests**

- `versioning.publish-transitions.spec.js`: Replaced stub tests with real assertions; added 4 new date validation tests covering semantically invalid dates (month 13, Feb 30 in both MM/DD/YYYY and ISO format).
- `aggregation.trimmed-mean.spec.js`: Added tests for `trimEnabled: false`, exact-boundary cases, single-score, symmetric distribution, and variance below threshold.
- `scoring.composite.spec.js`: Added weight validation tests via actual `saveDraft` calls; tests confirm that weights summing to 99.5 now throw (was silently allowed by `Math.round`) and that 99.995 passes within the epsilon.
- `idempotency.dedup.spec.js`: Updated mock to use the new `reserve`/`complete`/`deletePending` API; added test for pending-slot cleanup on handler failure and 409 IN_FLIGHT response.

Several issues from the audit remain open.
